Decision systems built around what leaders need to decide

Decision Intelligence Assessment

Who we are

We build the operating layer between business data and leadership action.

Not more disconnected dashboards or tools. One clearer way to see the business, decide what matters and act.

What changed?

Move with confidence.

Decision Intelligence Architects. We connect data, AI, automation and human judgment into one repeatable decision system.

See how we think
Our belief system

Why leaderschoose us

Every metric, model and automation should make an important decision clearer, faster or more reliable.

01

Decisions before dashboards

A dashboard has value only when it changes what someone does next. We design every view around a real business decision.

02

Truth before vanity metrics

Metrics should reveal reality, not create comfort. We define measures that leaders can trust, question and act on.

03

Predict, don't just report

Reporting explains yesterday. Decision Intelligence helps leaders anticipate what is changing and choose the next action.

04

AI augments human judgment

AI should surface patterns, risks and possibilities. People remain responsible for context, trade-offs and the final decision.

Before we begin

Decision Intelligence FAQs

Decision Intelligence is the practice of combining data, AI, automation and human judgment to improve how organisations make important decisions.
Business Intelligence shows what happened. Analytics explains why. AI identifies patterns, risks and possibilities. Decision Intelligence brings those signals together and helps leaders decide what to do next.
We work with founders, CEOs, COOs, CMOs, sales leaders and operations heads, especially in companies with roughly 20 to 500 employees that have outgrown spreadsheets but are not yet enterprise.
We help when reports arrive too late, teams work from different numbers, important signals are buried, or leaders cannot see which action will create the greatest impact.
The assessment maps decision bottlenecks, audits KPIs and reporting, identifies AI and automation opportunities, and produces a prioritised roadmap for what to improve first.
Not by default. We first diagnose what is useful, what is creating noise and where decisions are breaking down. Existing tools can remain where they support the right decisions.
AI is used to amplify human capability, not replace judgment. It surfaces patterns, risks and possibilities, and automates repetitive work while leaders remain responsible for the final decision.
Leaders should gain a clearer view of the business, faster movement from insight to action, and a repeatable system for making important decisions.
Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap slowing your business.

Book an Assessment

Who we are

We build decision systems for leaders who need clarity, not more noise.

Analytics Alt connects data, AI, automation and human judgment so growing businesses can see what matters, decide faster and act with confidence.

Better decisions compound.

Good decisions should not depend on one person or one moment. Our work turns knowledge, metrics and judgment into systems the business can reuse.

Mission

Turn information into confident action.

Our mission is to help growing businesses connect data, AI, automation and human judgment into a practical system for better decisions.

Vision

Make better decision-making a repeatable business capability.

Our vision is a future where every leader has a reliable thinking partner and every company can move from information to action with confidence.

Manifesto

What we believe

The future belongs to companies that think better, not simply faster.
Dashboards have value only when they change a decision or behaviour.
AI should amplify human judgment rather than replace it.
Knowledge should become a reusable system, not disappear when an engagement ends.
Complexity should be turned into clear, repeatable systems.
Every business leader deserves a reliable thinking partner.
Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap slowing your business.

Healthcare · Client identity withheld

How a healthcare brand increased monthly leads by 27% while lowering acquisition cost.

Lead volume had been declining for four years even as media spend increased. We rebuilt the acquisition system around clearer patient intent, stronger landing pages and disciplined campaign structure.

HealthcarePatient acquisitionGoogle Ads
Headline outcome
+27%more monthly leads on a lower budget

Before and after

The engagement focused on changing the operating system behind growth, not adding another reporting layer.

Before
₹129Cost per click
400Clicks per day
360Leads per month
After
₹40Cost per click
2,000Clicks per day
450Leads per month
Issue

What was holding growth back

The business was spending roughly ₹40K per day while leads declined by 6–8% each year for four years. A previous agency increased spend to about ₹1.5M per month, but performance remained flat and budget leakage kept compounding.

Approach

What changed

We restructured Google Ads, launched disruptive Display campaigns, deployed exit heatmaps and replaced weak landing pages with testimonial-led pages built around clearer patient intent.

Measured outcomes

The system created visible commercial impact.

-69%Cost per click
70%Lead to patient
₹60KSaved per month

Next: building a $13.1M B2B pipeline through cold email.

View next case study →

B2B Exhibition Services · Client identity withheld

How a B2B exhibition services company generated a $13.1M pipeline through cold email.

The company had no outbound email engine and depended entirely on paid media and referrals. We designed a segmented, repeatable system that created a new source of demand without compromising deliverability.

B2BTradeshow exhibitsCold email
Headline outcome
$13.1Mpipeline generated through cold email

Before and after

The engagement focused on changing the operating system behind growth, not adding another reporting layer.

Before
0Email outreach
100%Paid dependent
$0Email pipeline
After
1.1MEmails sent
74.9%Open rate
$13.1MPipeline value
Issue

What was holding growth back

The company was 100% dependent on paid ads and referrals for new business. There was no outbound pipeline, limited channel diversity and no repeatable way to create demand in the tradeshow exhibit market.

Approach

What changed

We built a 44-account cold-email system using Instantly.ai, deployed segmented campaigns with multi-step sequences, tested messaging angles and scaled outreach while protecting deliverability.

Measured outcomes

The system created visible commercial impact.

164Opportunities
~20%Lead to customer
74.9%Open rate

Next: increasing healthcare leads while reducing acquisition cost.

View next case study →

Decision Intelligence for SaaS

Turn recurring-revenue data into clearer growth decisions.

SaaS businesses generate metrics across acquisition, product, revenue and customer success. The challenge is not collecting more numbers. It is knowing which signals explain durable growth, which risks need attention and what the leadership team should do next.

The leadership questionAre we growing in a way that is efficient, predictable and likely to last?
The decision gap

A single growth number can hide several different businesses.

ARR can rise while activation weakens, acquisition becomes less efficient or future churn builds quietly inside the customer base.

We connect commercial, product and customer data so leaders can see the full growth system, identify the decision that matters now and move before a lagging metric confirms the problem.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which customer segments create the most durable recurring revenue?

02

Where does onboarding lose momentum before users reach value?

03

Is pipeline quality strong enough to support the forecast?

04

What behaviours are increasing churn or reducing expansion?

05

Which product signals predict retention and account growth?

06

Where should the next unit of growth investment go?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Blended growth metrics

Company-wide averages hide the difference between high-quality growth and revenue that is expensive or fragile.

Churn discovered too late

Teams react after a cancellation instead of identifying declining engagement and account risk earlier.

Disconnected revenue teams

Sales, product and customer success often work from separate definitions of customer health and opportunity.

Optimistic forecasting

Forecasts rely on stage labels and judgment without enough evidence from conversion, usage and account behaviour.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Net revenue retention

Whether the existing customer base is compounding or contracting.

Activation and time to value

How quickly new customers reach the behaviour that makes the product useful.

Customer acquisition payback

How efficiently acquisition spend returns as gross profit.

Pipeline quality and coverage

Whether future revenue is supported by enough credible opportunities.

Product adoption depth

Which features and workflows are connected to retention and expansion.

Forecast variance

Where expected growth repeatedly differs from actual performance.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the growth decisions, data sources and definitions used across revenue, product and customer teams.

02

Diagnose

Find the gaps between reported growth and the underlying quality, efficiency and retention signals.

03

Design

Create an executive KPI architecture that connects acquisition, activation, retention and expansion.

04

Decide

Prioritise the segment, product behaviour or commercial action with the greatest expected impact.

05

Drive

Build alerts, workflows and AI-supported analysis that keep the decision system active every week.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Executive SaaS scorecard

One trusted view of growth quality, efficiency, retention and forecast confidence.

Revenue intelligence layer

A connected model across CRM, billing, product usage and customer success data.

Churn and expansion signals

Early indicators that focus teams on the accounts most likely to contract or grow.

AI business analyst

A thinking partner that explains movement, surfaces risks and prepares the next questions for leadership.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap limiting predictable SaaS growth.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for Agencies

See which clients, services and teams are actually creating profitable growth.

Agencies can look busy while margins erode through scope creep, under-utilised talent, weak pricing or an unreliable pipeline. Decision Intelligence gives leaders a clearer view of capacity, client economics and the choices that protect both growth and delivery quality.

The leadership questionAre we building a profitable agency, or simply creating more work?
The decision gap

Activity is visible. Agency economics often are not.

Revenue, project delivery, time tracking and pipeline data usually live in different systems. By the time finance reports the margin, the decisions that created it have already happened.

We connect those signals around practical leadership decisions: what to sell, what to price differently, where capacity is tight and which client relationships deserve attention.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which clients and services generate healthy contribution margin?

02

Where is scope expanding without a matching commercial decision?

03

Do we have the right capacity for the work likely to close?

04

Which teams are overextended, under-utilised or poorly matched?

05

How much revenue is exposed to one client or channel?

06

What should we stop, standardise or productise?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Revenue without margin

Top-line growth can conceal expensive delivery, senior-team overuse and unbilled work.

Capacity seen too late

Hiring and freelance decisions happen after workloads become urgent instead of before demand arrives.

Pipeline without delivery context

Sales forecasts rarely show whether the agency can profitably fulfil the work being pursued.

Client health by instinct

Relationship strength is judged informally, even when payment, delivery and engagement signals show rising risk.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Gross margin by client

Which relationships create sustainable value after delivery cost.

Utilisation and realisation

How available time converts into billable and collectible revenue.

Scope variance

Where delivered effort is moving beyond the commercial agreement.

Pipeline-to-capacity ratio

Whether likely demand fits the team available to deliver it.

Client concentration

How dependent the business is on a small number of accounts.

Forecast accuracy

How reliably projected revenue and workload match reality.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the decisions behind pricing, staffing, delivery and account growth.

02

Diagnose

Identify where margin, capacity and pipeline information arrive too late or disagree.

03

Design

Build a management model connecting commercial demand with delivery economics.

04

Decide

Choose the client, service, pricing or resource action that protects profitable growth.

05

Drive

Automate weekly visibility, exceptions and ownership so issues do not depend on heroic intervention.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Agency profitability cockpit

Client, service and project economics in one decision-ready view.

Capacity planning system

Forward visibility into workload, skills, hiring and freelance requirements.

Scope and delivery alerts

Early warning when effort, timelines or margin move outside agreed boundaries.

Leadership review rhythm

A repeatable operating cadence that turns weekly numbers into clear actions and owners.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap between agency growth and agency profit.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for E-commerce

Make growth decisions using contribution, customer and inventory truth.

E-commerce teams can increase orders while weakening cash flow and profitability. Decision Intelligence connects acquisition, merchandising, retention, inventory and fulfilment so leaders understand not only what sold, but what created value and what should happen next.

The leadership questionWhich growth is profitable after acquisition, discounting, fulfilment and returns?
The decision gap

Revenue is easy to celebrate. Contribution is harder to see.

Channel dashboards optimise clicks and conversions. Store reports show sales. Inventory systems show stock. None of them alone explains whether a campaign, product or customer cohort created healthy economic value.

We create one decision layer across the customer journey and operating model, helping leaders balance demand, margin, cash and service.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which channels and campaigns create profitable new customers?

02

Which products drive repeat purchase rather than one-time revenue?

03

Where are discounts creating volume but destroying contribution?

04

Which inventory decisions are tying up cash or causing stock-outs?

05

What is driving returns, cancellations or fulfilment cost?

06

Which customer cohorts deserve more investment?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

ROAS without contribution

Advertising performance can appear strong before product margin, discounts, shipping and returns are included.

Customer value averaged

High-value repeat customers and promotion-led one-time buyers are blended into the same reporting.

Inventory disconnected from demand

Merchandising and marketing decisions are made without a shared view of stock risk and cash exposure.

Promotion learning lost

Teams repeat campaigns without a clear record of who bought, what they bought later and whether the offer created value.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Contribution margin

The value left after variable product, acquisition and fulfilment costs.

New-customer payback

How quickly acquisition investment returns through customer contribution.

Repeat purchase rate

Whether cohorts develop into durable customer relationships.

Inventory cover and ageing

Where stock is likely to run out or remain unsold.

Return-adjusted product margin

Product economics after returns, refunds and handling costs.

Promotion incrementality

Whether an offer created additional demand or discounted demand that already existed.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map commercial decisions across marketing, merchandising, inventory and fulfilment.

02

Diagnose

Find where revenue reporting hides contribution, cash or customer-quality problems.

03

Design

Connect order, advertising, product, customer and inventory data around the decisions leaders make.

04

Decide

Prioritise the channel, product, cohort or inventory action with the strongest business case.

05

Drive

Create automated signals for margin leakage, stock risk, customer change and campaign performance.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

E-commerce executive scorecard

A single view of revenue quality, contribution, cash and customer health.

Channel profitability model

Performance by channel and campaign after the full cost of acquiring and serving demand.

Customer cohort intelligence

Retention, repeat behaviour and value patterns that guide acquisition and lifecycle decisions.

Inventory decision system

Forward signals connecting demand, stock position, ageing and cash exposure.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap between e-commerce revenue and profitable growth.

Decision Intelligence for Healthcare

Give healthcare leaders a clearer view of access, capacity and operational performance.

Healthcare organisations make daily decisions that affect patient access, team workload, service quality and financial sustainability. Decision Intelligence connects operational, commercial and service data so leaders can identify constraints earlier and act with greater confidence.

The leadership questionWhere is the system preventing patients, clinicians and operations from working better together?
The decision gap

Healthcare data is abundant, but the operational picture is often fragmented.

Patient acquisition, scheduling, clinical operations, billing and workforce systems answer different questions. Leaders are left reconciling reports while access problems, no-shows, capacity gaps and revenue-cycle delays continue.

We build a decision layer around the organisation's management priorities. The aim is not more reporting. It is earlier visibility, clearer ownership and more reliable action.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Where are patients waiting, dropping out or failing to convert?

02

Which locations, services or time periods have unused capacity?

03

What is driving no-shows, cancellations and rescheduling?

04

Where are workforce constraints affecting service delivery?

05

Which acquisition sources produce appropriate, retained patients?

06

Where are billing and collection delays weakening cash flow?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Access measured in fragments

Enquiries, bookings, attendance and treatment are reported separately rather than as one patient-access journey.

Capacity hidden by averages

Organisation-wide utilisation can conceal local bottlenecks and underused appointment capacity.

Acquisition without service fit

Marketing is judged by lead volume instead of booked, attended and appropriate patient demand.

Operational issues reviewed late

Problems become visible in monthly reports after patients and teams have already experienced them.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Enquiry-to-attendance conversion

How effectively patient demand moves through booking and attendance.

Capacity utilisation

Where available clinical and operational capacity is used or lost.

No-show and cancellation patterns

Which services, cohorts and lead times carry the greatest attendance risk.

Patient acquisition efficiency

The cost and quality of demand through to an attended appointment.

Revenue-cycle timing

How quickly delivered services move through billing and collection.

Service-level variance

Where locations or teams perform differently and require investigation.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the management decisions connecting patient access, capacity, workforce and financial operations.

02

Diagnose

Identify where fragmented systems delay visibility or create conflicting definitions.

03

Design

Create a trusted KPI architecture around the patient journey and operating constraints.

04

Decide

Focus leaders on the access, capacity or process intervention with the greatest expected effect.

05

Drive

Embed alerts, reviews and accountable workflows while preserving human and clinical judgment.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Healthcare operations cockpit

Decision-ready visibility across demand, appointments, capacity and service performance.

Patient access intelligence

A connected view from first enquiry through booking, attendance and follow-up.

Capacity and workforce signals

Earlier warning of underuse, overload and operational bottlenecks.

Executive review system

A repeatable leadership rhythm for moving from operational evidence to owned action.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap affecting healthcare access and operations.

Decision Intelligence for Finance

Move from backward-looking finance reports to forward-looking business decisions.

Finance leaders are expected to explain performance, protect cash, manage risk and guide the next move. Decision Intelligence turns financial and operational information into a shared management system for understanding what changed, why it changed and what action is justified.

The leadership questionWhat does the financial truth tell us to do next?
The decision gap

Finance should be the decision engine of the business, not only its record keeper.

Monthly accounts explain the result after the period has closed. Leaders also need earlier signals from sales, operations, customers and cash to understand where the result is heading.

We connect financial outcomes with their operational drivers, giving finance and executive teams a common view of performance, risk and trade-offs.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which operational drivers are changing revenue, margin and cash?

02

Where is forecast confidence weakest and why?

03

Which customers, products or services create economic value?

04

What commitments are likely to pressure future cash flow?

05

Where are exceptions, leakage or control failures appearing?

06

Which scenario gives leaders the strongest risk-adjusted choice?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Financial results without drivers

Leaders see the variance but cannot trace it quickly to the operational behaviour that caused it.

Forecasts updated manually

Planning depends on slow spreadsheet cycles and assumptions that are difficult to challenge.

Profitability averaged

Customer, product and service economics disappear inside company-level totals.

Risk buried in reports

Exceptions and control issues are found through review rather than surfaced when action is still possible.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Cash conversion

How operating performance turns into available cash.

Forecast variance

Where actual outcomes differ from expectations and which assumptions failed.

Contribution by customer or offer

The economic value created below headline revenue.

Working-capital movement

How receivables, payables and inventory affect liquidity.

Cost-to-serve

Where operational complexity changes the real profitability of revenue.

Exception frequency

Which controls, transactions or processes repeatedly require intervention.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the decisions finance supports and the operational evidence each decision requires.

02

Diagnose

Find manual bottlenecks, weak definitions and gaps between financial outcomes and business drivers.

03

Design

Build an integrated management model for performance, cash, profitability and risk.

04

Decide

Compare scenarios and focus leadership on the action with the clearest economic logic.

05

Drive

Automate recurring analysis, exceptions and decision follow-through without removing accountable judgment.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Executive finance cockpit

A concise view of performance, cash, forecast and business drivers.

Driver-based forecasting

Planning models that connect operational assumptions to financial outcomes.

Profitability intelligence

Customer, product and service economics that reveal where value is created or lost.

Exception and control alerts

Automated visibility into unusual movement, leakage and recurring process failure.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap between financial reporting and business action.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for Real Estate

Connect demand, portfolio and operational data to better real-estate decisions.

Real-estate leaders balance pricing, occupancy, pipeline, capital, service and asset performance. Decision Intelligence creates one management view across the portfolio so teams can identify where value is changing and choose the next action with confidence.

The leadership questionWhich asset, market or operating decision will create the greatest portfolio impact?
The decision gap

Portfolio averages hide the places where value is gained or lost.

Leads may rise while conversion falls. Occupancy can look stable while concessions increase. Maintenance cost can grow without a clear asset-level explanation.

We connect commercial and operating signals at the level where decisions are actually made: market, property, unit, project, channel and customer segment.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which properties and units are underperforming their opportunity?

02

Where is lead quality or sales conversion weakening?

03

How should pricing change by asset, market or demand condition?

04

Which maintenance issues are affecting cost and customer experience?

05

Where is capital likely to create the strongest return?

06

Which pipeline assumptions are most exposed to delay or change?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Portfolio averages

Strong assets can conceal weak occupancy, margin or service performance elsewhere.

Lead volume without intent

Marketing reports demand but not the quality, conversion and economic value of that demand.

Reactive asset operations

Maintenance and service problems are handled individually without learning from recurring patterns.

Static planning

Pricing and investment choices rely on periodic reviews rather than current demand and operating evidence.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Occupancy and vacancy movement

Where utilisation is changing and how quickly vacant capacity converts.

Lead-to-close conversion

How demand progresses by channel, market, asset and team.

Net effective pricing

The real commercial outcome after concessions and incentives.

Operating cost by asset

Where cost intensity differs and requires management attention.

Maintenance recurrence

Which issues repeatedly affect service, cost and asset condition.

Pipeline confidence

How likely projects, leases or sales are to occur within the expected period.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the portfolio decisions made across commercial, asset and operational teams.

02

Diagnose

Find where aggregated reporting or disconnected systems hide local performance.

03

Design

Create a property and portfolio KPI architecture tied to pricing, occupancy, service and capital decisions.

04

Decide

Prioritise the asset, market or intervention with the strongest evidence and expected impact.

05

Drive

Use automated signals and review workflows to keep portfolio action current and accountable.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Portfolio intelligence cockpit

Performance and risk visibility from portfolio level down to individual assets.

Demand and conversion model

A connected view of channel, lead quality, sales activity and commercial outcome.

Asset operations intelligence

Patterns across maintenance, service, cost and recurring property issues.

Pricing and scenario support

Decision models that compare demand, occupancy, incentives and expected value.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap limiting real-estate portfolio performance.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for Logistics

Turn operational movement into faster, more reliable logistics decisions.

Logistics businesses operate through thousands of connected choices about capacity, routes, service, cost and exceptions. Decision Intelligence helps leaders see where the network is changing, why performance is moving and which intervention will protect margin and customer reliability.

The leadership questionWhere should we intervene before a service problem becomes a customer problem?
The decision gap

The network produces constant data. Leaders need prioritised exceptions.

Shipment, fleet, warehouse, workforce and customer systems can describe activity without revealing the constraint that matters most.

We create a decision layer that connects service outcomes with their operational and commercial drivers, helping teams act on the few exceptions that deserve attention.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Where are delays and service failures beginning to concentrate?

02

Which routes, customers or services are eroding margin?

03

Do forecast volumes match available network capacity?

04

What is driving empty movement, dwell time or low utilisation?

05

Which exceptions require intervention now rather than later?

06

Where should process, pricing or capacity change first?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Activity without priority

Operations teams receive many alerts but little guidance on which exception creates the greatest business risk.

Service and cost separated

On-time performance is reviewed apart from the cost required to achieve it.

Customer profitability unclear

Revenue is visible, but route complexity, exceptions and service effort are not fully attributed.

Capacity decisions lag demand

Resources are adjusted after congestion or underutilisation has already affected performance.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

On-time performance

Service reliability by route, customer, location and operating condition.

Cost per movement

The operating cost of delivering each shipment, route or service.

Capacity utilisation

How effectively fleet, warehouse and labour capacity are used.

Exception resolution time

How quickly disruption moves from detection to owned action.

Customer contribution

Commercial value after the real cost and complexity of service.

Forecast-to-capacity variance

Where expected demand and available resources are moving apart.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the decisions connecting demand, capacity, routing, service and customer commitments.

02

Diagnose

Identify recurring constraints, delayed information and measures that optimise one function at another's expense.

03

Design

Build a network KPI architecture around reliability, cost, capacity and exception management.

04

Decide

Rank interventions by operational urgency, customer impact and economic value.

05

Drive

Automate alerts, ownership and review so the network learns from every recurring exception.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Logistics control tower

A decision-ready view of service, cost, capacity and network exceptions.

Route and customer economics

Profitability visibility that includes the operational complexity of fulfilment.

Exception prioritisation

Signals that separate routine noise from issues requiring immediate management action.

Capacity intelligence

Forward visibility that connects forecast demand with fleet, warehouse and workforce needs.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap affecting logistics reliability and margin.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for Education

Connect enrolment, engagement and operations to better education decisions.

Education organisations need to balance learner outcomes, enrolment, retention, capacity and financial sustainability. Decision Intelligence helps leaders understand where the learner journey is changing and which action can improve both experience and performance.

The leadership questionWhere is the learner journey breaking, and what should we change first?
The decision gap

Education data often describes stages instead of the complete learner journey.

Marketing sees enquiries. Admissions sees applications. Academic teams see engagement. Finance sees fees. Leaders need those views connected before they can explain enrolment, retention or capacity.

We build a shared decision system that respects educational judgment while making operational evidence easier to see and act on.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which channels and programmes attract learners who enrol and persist?

02

Where do applicants stall or leave the admissions journey?

03

Which engagement patterns signal rising retention risk?

04

How should staff, timetable and facility capacity be allocated?

05

Which programmes are creating educational and financial value?

06

Where can learner support have the greatest effect?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Enrolment funnel fragmented

Enquiries, applications, offers and starts are measured by separate teams with inconsistent definitions.

Retention seen after withdrawal

Risk becomes visible through a final outcome instead of earlier engagement and support signals.

Programme performance averaged

Organisation-level results hide differences in demand, capacity, learner experience and economics.

Support not prioritised

Teams know many learners may need help but lack a clear, responsible way to focus attention.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Enquiry-to-enrolment conversion

How demand progresses through the admissions journey.

Learner engagement

Participation patterns that help teams investigate support needs earlier.

Retention and progression

Whether learners continue and move successfully through their programme.

Programme contribution

The operational and financial sustainability of each offer.

Capacity utilisation

How effectively teaching, staff and facilities support demand.

Support response time

How quickly identified learner needs move to appropriate human action.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map leadership decisions across recruitment, admissions, delivery, support and planning.

02

Diagnose

Find where disconnected stages or delayed reporting prevent an accurate learner view.

03

Design

Create a KPI architecture connecting demand, enrolment, engagement, outcomes and capacity.

04

Decide

Focus resources on the programme, stage or learner-support issue with the clearest evidence.

05

Drive

Embed responsible alerts and workflows that support, rather than replace, educator judgment.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Education leadership cockpit

A shared view of enrolment, retention, capacity and programme performance.

Admissions intelligence

Connected funnel visibility from first enquiry through confirmed start.

Learner engagement signals

Earlier patterns that help authorised teams prioritise appropriate support.

Programme planning model

Decision support for demand, delivery capacity and financial sustainability.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap affecting enrolment, retention or education operations.

Decision Intelligence for Manufacturing

Make production decisions with one view of throughput, quality and constraint.

Manufacturing performance depends on connected decisions across demand, materials, machines, labour, quality and delivery. Decision Intelligence helps leaders identify the constraint behind the number and choose the action that improves the whole system, not only one department.

The leadership questionWhat is the real constraint on profitable, reliable output?
The decision gap

Local efficiency can improve while total performance gets worse.

A machine can run faster while work-in-progress rises. Inventory can protect service while consuming cash. Output can meet plan while quality losses grow.

We connect operational and commercial evidence so leaders can see trade-offs, distinguish symptoms from constraints and coordinate action across the production system.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which constraint is limiting throughput right now?

02

What is driving downtime, scrap, rework or schedule loss?

03

Do demand, materials and capacity support the production plan?

04

Where is inventory protecting service and where is it hiding problems?

05

Which products, customers or changeovers create avoidable complexity?

06

What action will improve output without weakening quality or margin?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Department metrics conflict

Functions optimise utilisation, inventory or output separately even when the total system suffers.

Downtime without cause patterns

Events are recorded but recurring combinations of machine, product, shift and condition remain hidden.

Schedule risk seen late

Material, capacity and quality signals are not connected early enough to protect delivery.

Cost detached from operations

Financial variance is reviewed without a fast path to the production behaviour that created it.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Throughput at the constraint

The rate at which the limiting part of the system creates saleable output.

Downtime and changeover loss

Where available production time is repeatedly consumed.

First-pass yield

How much output meets requirements without rework.

Schedule attainment

Whether planned production is completed in the expected sequence and period.

Inventory health

Where materials and finished goods support flow or tie up cash.

Cost of poor quality

The operational and financial impact of scrap, rework and failure.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the decisions linking demand, planning, materials, production, quality and delivery.

02

Diagnose

Find the constraint, data gaps and local measures that distort system-wide decisions.

03

Design

Create a production KPI architecture connecting throughput, reliability, quality and cash.

04

Decide

Choose the intervention with the strongest effect on the total operating system.

05

Drive

Use automated signals, review routines and ownership to sustain improvement beyond individual heroes.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Manufacturing performance cockpit

One management view of plan, throughput, downtime, quality and delivery risk.

Constraint intelligence

A focused model showing where flow is limited and what is driving the loss.

Quality and downtime patterns

Connected analysis across product, machine, shift, material and process conditions.

Production decision workflows

Repeatable alerts and action ownership for schedule, material and operating exceptions.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap limiting manufacturing throughput and reliability.

Book an Assessment

Decision Intelligence for Hospitality

Connect demand, guest experience and operations into one hospitality decision system.

Hospitality leaders make daily trade-offs across pricing, occupancy, channels, labour, service and cost. Decision Intelligence gives them a clearer view of what is changing across properties and where action can improve both guest experience and commercial performance.

The leadership questionWhat should we change today to protect tomorrow's guest experience and profit?
The decision gap

A strong occupancy number does not explain the quality of the business behind it.

Demand can grow through expensive channels. Rates can rise while guest sentiment weakens. Labour can be reduced while service failures increase.

We connect commercial and operational signals so leaders can see the full consequence of pricing, staffing, channel and service decisions.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which channels and segments create the strongest net revenue?

02

Where should pricing change as demand conditions move?

03

Do staffing levels match expected occupancy and service demand?

04

What is driving guest complaints, recovery cost or weak reviews?

05

Which properties, outlets or services are underperforming?

06

Where is cost control damaging the guest experience?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Occupancy without channel economics

Room demand is celebrated before commissions, discounts and guest value are understood.

Guest feedback reviewed separately

Service issues are not connected quickly enough to staffing, property, shift or process conditions.

Labour planned from history

Schedules rely on past patterns without enough connection to current bookings and service demand.

Property averages

Group-level performance can conceal local commercial and operating problems.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Net revenue by channel

Commercial value after channel and promotion costs.

Occupancy and rate mix

How demand, pricing and segment composition interact.

Revenue per available capacity

The productivity of rooms or other sellable hospitality capacity.

Labour-to-demand alignment

Whether staffing matches expected occupancy and service workload.

Guest issue recurrence

Where service problems repeatedly appear by property, shift or process.

Outlet and service contribution

Which additional experiences create sustainable value.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map the decisions behind pricing, demand, staffing, service and property performance.

02

Diagnose

Identify where commercial and operating reports describe different versions of reality.

03

Design

Build a hospitality KPI architecture connecting demand quality, guest experience, labour and margin.

04

Decide

Prioritise the pricing, staffing, channel or service action with the clearest expected impact.

05

Drive

Create daily and weekly signals that turn changing conditions into owned operational action.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Hospitality executive cockpit

Property and group visibility across demand, rate, channel, labour and guest experience.

Channel and segment economics

A clear view of net commercial value by source and customer segment.

Guest experience intelligence

Patterns connecting feedback and service recovery to operating conditions.

Demand-led workforce planning

Forward staffing guidance linked to bookings, occupancy and service workload.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap affecting hospitality performance and guest experience.

Decision Intelligence for Professional Services

Build a professional-services firm that can see demand, capacity and value clearly.

Professional-services firms grow through judgment, expertise and trusted relationships. But leadership decisions about pipeline, pricing, staffing and delivery still need reliable evidence. Decision Intelligence turns scattered operational data into a repeatable system for profitable growth.

The leadership questionAre we deploying the right expertise on the right work at the right economics?
The decision gap

Expert businesses should not depend on invisible management knowledge.

Partners and senior leaders often carry the real operating picture in their heads. Pipeline confidence, client health, capacity and delivery risk can remain informal until a problem becomes urgent.

We make that intelligence visible and reusable without reducing complex professional judgment to a simplistic dashboard.

Questions leaders need answered

Better decisions begin with better business questions.

We organise data around the decisions leadership must make, not around the systems where the data happens to live.

01

Which clients, matters or engagements create sustainable value?

02

Does the likely pipeline fit available expertise and capacity?

03

Where are pricing, write-offs or delivery effort weakening margin?

04

Which client relationships carry concentration or retention risk?

05

Where is senior expertise becoming a bottleneck?

06

What knowledge and delivery patterns should become repeatable systems?

Common blind spots

Where visibility usually breaks down.

The problem is rarely a complete lack of information. It is fragmented truth, late signals and measures that do not lead to action.

Pipeline confidence is personal

Forecast quality depends on individual partner judgment without shared evidence or definitions.

Utilisation without value

Busy teams can still produce weak realisation, margin or client outcomes.

Delivery risk emerges late

Scope, staffing and milestone concerns are discussed after economics or relationships have deteriorated.

Knowledge remains with individuals

The firm repeatedly solves similar problems without converting learning into reusable methods.

Decision-ready measures

Metrics should explain reality and guide action.

The final KPI architecture is tailored to the organisation. These are the types of measures that often matter when leadership needs a clearer operating view.

Engagement contribution

The value created after the actual cost and seniority of delivery.

Utilisation and realisation

How professional capacity turns into billable and collected value.

Pipeline confidence

The probability, timing and resource implications of future work.

Client concentration and health

Where revenue and relationship risk are accumulating.

Scope and milestone variance

Where work is moving beyond the agreed plan.

Cash collection cycle

How quickly delivered professional value converts into cash.

Decision Intelligence Framework™

From disconnected information to repeatable action.

Our five-stage method combines data, AI, automation and human judgment without allowing the technology to replace accountable leadership.

01

Discover

Map decisions across business development, pricing, staffing, delivery and client management.

02

Diagnose

Find where informal judgment, delayed data or inconsistent definitions create avoidable risk.

03

Design

Build an executive KPI architecture connecting demand, capacity, delivery and economics.

04

Decide

Focus leaders on the client, engagement, staffing or pricing action with the greatest impact.

05

Drive

Create a repeatable review and knowledge system that scales beyond exceptional individuals.

What we build

A decision system, not another isolated dashboard.

The right solution may include KPI architecture, integrated business intelligence, automation and an AI-supported thinking layer. The design starts with the decision, not the tool.

Firm performance cockpit

One decision-ready view of pipeline, capacity, delivery, margin and cash.

Engagement economics

Clear profitability and delivery visibility by client, matter, project or service line.

Capacity and expertise planning

Forward alignment between likely work and the people required to deliver it.

Institutional knowledge system

Reusable frameworks, signals and review routines that turn expertise into organisational capability.

Start with an assessment

Find the decision gap limiting professional-services growth and delivery.
